title: Ducrosia areysiana
taxonomy: Ducrosia areysiana is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Apiaceae, commonly known as the carrot or parsley family. It belongs to the genus Ducrosia and is native to the Middle East and North Africa. The plant is further classified as a dicotyledonous herb, characterized by its distinctive leaf and flower morphology. Ducrosia areysiana is also classified as a member of the tribe Scandiceae, a group of plants with similar characteristics. Further taxonomic classification places it within the subfamily Apioideae, which includes many other species of plants with similar leaf and flower structures.

intro: Ducrosia areysiana, also known as Ducrosia, is a small shrub plant native to the Middle East and North Africa. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, inflammation, and digestive problems, due to its potential health benefits in reducing pain and promoting wound healing. Ducrosia areysiana contains bioactive constituents such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and coumarins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
